free_text: Precision determination of charge dispersion and
distribution in the proton-induced fission of U-238
at 13.9 MeV excitation
E-Mail regarding to misprint.
Decay data. ENSDF was used
too.
The targets of U-238 were prepared by electrodeposition
of uranium dioxide on aluminum foils.
* For the measurement of products of the half-life
shorter than 30 min down to seconds, authors made use
of gas-jet transport system. The target thickness of
uranium was 1.0 mg/cm**2.
* For the measurement of products of half-life longer
than 10 min, the target of 1.5-2.0 mg/cm2 in uranium
thickness was sandwiched by aluminum catcher foils or
graphite foils with appropriate thickness. A stack of
the target and catcher foils were irradiated at the end
of the beam course. The irradiation durations were 30
min for short-lived products and 3 hr for long-lived
products.
The independent and cumulative yields were computed
with the correction for the growth and decay during
bombardment.
The total chain yields are reported.
For products with half-life longer 10 min.
Antimony, iodine, and rare earth isotopes were
isolated from the other fission products in separate
runs by the chemical separation.
The beam intensity from the accelerator was in
the range of 500 nA to 1 uA for all the experiments
including the gas-jet experiment.
For the measurement of products of the half-
life shorter than 30 min down to seconds.
For short-lived isotopes.
Due to statistical errors in counting the
gamma-ray emission and expected errors in the
photoemission probability.
Tbl.1 from Zeitschr.Phys.,356(1996)55
Correction some data.
Last checking has been done.

- By Compiler - Original paper contains the mistake
for independent yield of Sr-91. Private communication
of Yokoyama corrected it.
- By Yokoyana - Regarding to the cumulative yield of 91
in mass, we admit that what you pointed out has some
problem. We re-checked the data on Sr-91. It was
found that wrong numbers were mixed somehow and the
independent yield of Sr-91 was 0.014+-0.046 instead of
However, as you see, the right data on Sr-91 has  a
large uncertainty. So we should have dropped the data
from the list.

SD: SF4=Zn-73g -> Zn-73 from REACTION code.
- By Compiler - These data contain a mistake, because
cumulative cross section of Zr-95 production can not be
less than cumulative cross section of Y-95.
- By a.Yokoyama - Regarding to Y-95 and Zr-95, there is
no  mistake  on   the   data.    Considering   on   the
relationship  of  parent  and  daughter, the cumulative
yield of Zr-95 should be more than that of Y-95.
As  we  determined  the  cumulative  yields  from   the
individual   formation   cross   sections   of  product
nuclides, the opposite situation may happen due to  the
uncertainty  of observed data.  We think that it is due
to the uncertainty of Y-95, which is based on one sigma
dispersion in our study.
SD: Y-97m -> Y-97m1 in REACTION,DECAY-DATA,
RAD-DET.
- By Compiler - According to ENSDF 50(3) sec activity
is 115-Pd isomer activity.
